rationale: The citing paper uses Chakravarti et al. (2006) to support a specific theoretical possibility about sponsored search behavior: that prescreening information may be deemphasized, implying conversion could be independent of ad position. This citation appears in the core literature framing (not just the reference list) as part of the mechanism-level discussion that motivates the paper's empirical question about how position affects conversions. Although it is one of several competing theories considered, it meaningfully informs the conceptual setup of the study.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Chakravarti et al.'s prescreening-neglect insight to sponsored search, using it to motivate the prediction that ad position may not affect post-click conversion. It then empirically tests this possibility against alternative primacy/recency-based accounts.
